title: manulea parviflora var. limonioides
intro: Manulea parviflora var. limonioides, also known as wild ginger, is a plant species that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, and is used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Manulea parviflora var. limonioides include relief from digestive issues, such as nausea and stomach discomfort, as well as its ability to reduce pain and fever. Manulea parviflora var. limonioides is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and can be used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
